title: Codonoboea kelantanensis
intro: Codonoboea kelantanensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to the tropical rainforests of Malaysia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Codonoboea kelantanensis include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Codonoboea kelantanensis is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Codonoboea kelantanensis is a species of plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is native to Malaysia, specifically in the state of Kelantan. The plant is classified as a flowering plant, belonging to the order Lamiales. Within the family Gesneriaceae, Codonoboea kelantanensis is classified in the genus Codonoboea. This classification indicates the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other species within the same genus and family.
